noveltyJustification: The router's existence was expected, but this pass found a plan-level design correction: model substitution context lives in fanout-run.cjs or a pool hook, not naturally inside the generic pool primitive.

findings.title: fallback-router is shipped and validates fallback graphs
router unit tests cover pure routing but not fanout GLM/MiMo integration
model-aware fallback belongs in fanout-run or an explicit pool hook
GLM to MiMo fallback is optional feature work, not merge-critical

answeredQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback, or remain optional/deferred?

ruledOut.approach: Treat fallback-router wiring as required for merge closeout
ruledOut.reason: The phase is P2 optional and needs config/schema/integration-test work unrelated to rename/path/reference migration.

ruledOut.approach: Wire model substitution directly into the generic pool without a hook
ruledOut.reason: fanout-pool.cjs only owns generic items/retries; model-aware command construction and lineage context live in fanout-run.cjs.
